title: Kang IFN-β PBMC: MOFA-FLEX from R to MOFA2
text: vignette demonstrates end--end MOFA-FLEX workflow entirely R: Load Kang et al. (2018) PBMC IFN-β stimulation dataset muscData. Preprocess standard Bioconductor tools (scran / scuttle). Bridge resulting SingleCellExperiment Python AnnData via sce_to_reticulate_anndata(). Train MOFA-FLEX model fit_mofaflex(), saving model HDF5 file compatible MOFA2. Load saved model MOFA2 R package downstream analysis.

title: Kang IFN-β PBMC: MOFA-FLEX from R to MOFA2
text: Remove multiplets low-quality cells normalisation.
code: ## Remove multiplets and cells without an assigned cell type sce <- sce_full[, sce_full$multiplets == "singlet" & !is.na(sce_full$cell)]  ## Keep cells with ≥ 200 detected genes qc <- scuttle::perCellQCMetrics(sce) sce <- sce[, qc$detected >= 200]  cat(sprintf("After QC: %d genes × %d cells\n", nrow(sce), ncol(sce))) #> After QC: 35635 genes × 24562 cells

title: Kang IFN-β PBMC: MOFA-FLEX from R to MOFA2
text: Log-normalise using pooling-based size factors scran.
code: set.seed(42) # clusters <- scran::quickCluster(sce) # sce      <- scran::computeSumFactors(sce, clusters = clusters) sce <- scuttle::logNormCounts(sce)  assayNames(sce) # should include "logcounts" #> [1] "counts"    "logcounts"

title: Kang IFN-β PBMC: MOFA-FLEX from R to MOFA2
text: Retain 3,000 variable genes keep model tractable.
code: dec <- scran::modelGeneVar(sce) hvgs <- scran::getTopHVGs(dec, n = 3000) sce_hvg <- sce[hvgs, ]  cat(sprintf("Using %d HVGs from %d cells.\n", nrow(sce_hvg), ncol(sce_hvg))) #> Using 3000 HVGs from 24562 cells.

title: Kang IFN-β PBMC: MOFA-FLEX from R to MOFA2
text: sce_to_reticulate_anndata() builds Python AnnData SCE without copying underlying sparse matrix (zero-copy bridge). colData columns forwarded adata.obs. stim column adata$obs ("ctrl" / "stim") used MOFA-FLEX split cells two groups.
code: adata <- sce_to_reticulate_anndata(     sce_hvg,     assay = "logcounts" ) adata #> ReticulateAnnData object with n_obs × n_vars = 24562 × 3000 #>     obs: 'ind', 'stim', 'cluster', 'cell', 'multiplets', 'sizeFactor' #>     var: 'ENSEMBL', 'SYMBOL'

title: Kang IFN-β PBMC: MOFA-FLEX from R to MOFA2
text: Key choices: group_by = "stim" — split cells ctrl stim groups using stim column obs. subset_var = NULL — HVGs already selected; skip highly_variable filter. save_path — write trained model HDF5 file. mofa_compat = "full" — save MOFA2-readable format. View name rna (automatic wrapping single AnnData). Compute time. modern CPU typically converges minutes thanks early stopping.
code: ## Output path — adjust to a stable directory in your project output_dir <- file.path("results", "mofaflex") dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE) model_path <- file.path(output_dir, "kang_mofaflex.hdf5") model <- fit_mofaflex(     data = adata,     data_options = list(         # group_by           = "stim",         scale_per_group    = TRUE,         subset_var         = NULL,         plot_data_overview = FALSE     ),     model_options = list(         n_factors    = 5L,         weight_prior = "Horseshoe"     ),     training_options = list(         max_epochs             = 5000L,         early_stopper_patience = 50L,         seed                   = 42L,         device                 = "cuda",  # or "cpu" if no GPU is available         save_path              = model_path     ),     mofa_compat = "full"  # write a MOFA2-readable HDF5 file )

title: Kang IFN-β PBMC: MOFA-FLEX from R to MOFA2
text: model saved mofa_compat = "full", HDF5 file contains MOFA2-compatible weight factor matrices. Use load_mofaflex_model() wrapper (handles minor HDF5-format differences mofaflex MOFA2 automatically) load .
code: mofa <- load_mofaflex_model(model_path) #> Warning in .quality_control(object, verbose = verbose): The model contains highly correlated factors (see `plot_factor_cor(MOFAobject)`).  #> We recommend that you train the model with less factors and that you let it train for a longer time.  ## Attach sample metadata so colour_by aesthetics work. ## MOFA2 requires a 'sample' column matching the model's cell IDs. cells_per_group <- MOFA2::samples_names(mofa) meta <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(cells_per_group), function(grp) {     cells <- cells_per_group[[grp]]     sdf <- as.data.frame(SummarizedExperiment::colData(sce_hvg)[cells, , drop = FALSE])     data.frame(         sample = cells, group = grp, sdf,         check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE     ) })) MOFA2::samples_metadata(mofa) <- meta mofa #> Trained MOFA with the following characteristics:  #>  Number of views: 1  #>  Views names: rna  #>  Number of features (per view): 3000  #>  Number of groups: 1  #>  Groups names: group_1  #>  Number of samples (per group): 24562  #>  Number of factors: 5

title: Kang IFN-β PBMC: MOFA-FLEX from R to MOFA2
text: Top 10 feature weights Factor 1. UMAP MOFA-FLEX factor scores coloured stimulation condition. Alternatively, use basilisk run MOFAFlex separate Python process. model saved disk anyway loaded MOFA2. setBasiliskShared(TRUE), Python model loaded current R session.
code: MOFA2::plot_top_weights(     mofa,     view      = "rna",     factor    = 5,     nfeatures = 10 ) factors <- MOFA2::get_factors(mofa, factors = 1:5) df <- cbind(     factors$group_1,     as.data.frame(SummarizedExperiment::colData(sce_hvg)[rownames(factors$group_1), ]) ) umaps <- uwot::umap(factors$group_1, n_neighbors = 15, min_dist = 0.1, metric = "cosine") df <- cbind(df, UMAP1 = umaps[, 1], UMAP2 = umaps[, 2]) ggplot2::ggplot(df, ggplot2::aes(x = UMAP1, y = UMAP2, color = stim)) +     ggplot2::geom_point(size = 0.5, alpha = 0.6) +     ggplot2::theme_minimal() +     ggplot2::labs(title = "UMAP of MOFA-FLEX factors coloured by stimulation condition") basilisk::setBasiliskFork(TRUE) # use forked processes for parallelism (Linux/Mac only) basilisk::setBasiliskShared(FALSE) # share the same Python environment across processes (saves memory)  output_dir <- file.path("results", "mofaflex") dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE) model_path <- file.path(output_dir, "kang_mofaflex.hdf5")  with_mofaflex_env({   adata <- MofaflexR::sce_to_reticulate_anndata(sce_hvg, assay = "logcounts")   MofaflexR::fit_mofaflex(     data = adata,     data_options = list(scale_per_group = TRUE, subset_var = NULL, plot_data_overview = FALSE),     model_options = list(n_factors = 5L, weight_prior = "Horseshoe"),     training_options = list(max_epochs = 5000L, early_stopper_patience = 50L, seed = 42L,                             device = "cuda", save_path = model_path),     mofa_compat = "modelonly"   )   return(NULL) }, sce_hvg = sce_hvg, model_path = model_path)

title: Bridging Single-Cell Multi-Omics: SCE and MAE to Python MuData
text: MofaflexR provides zero-copy bridges Bioconductor single-cell objects Python mudata / anndata ecosystem. vignette demonstrates: sce_to_reticulate_mudata(): Convert SingleCellExperiment – alternative experiments (altExps) encoding additional modalities surface-protein (ADT/CITE-seq) chromatin-accessibility (ATAC) – ReticulateMuData. mae_to_reticulate_mudata(): Convert MultiAssayExperiment multiple experiments ReticulateMuData. ReticulateMuData active bindings methods: Access Python MuData fields directly R without data copies. matrix transfers zero-copy: R dgCMatrix objects shared Python scipy.sparse.csc_matrix pointers. Python ↔︎ R serialisation takes place unless explicitly call reticulate::py_to_r().

title: Bridging Single-Cell Multi-Omics: SCE and MAE to Python MuData
code: # Ensure the mofaflex_test conda environment is available (or your own) reticulate::use_condaenv("mofaflex_test", required = TRUE)  # Or configure via ~/.Renviron: #   RETICULATE_PYTHON=/path/to/python suppressPackageStartupMessages({   library(MofaflexR)   library(SingleCellExperiment)   library(SummarizedExperiment)   library(Matrix)   library(S4Vectors) })

title: Bridging Single-Cell Multi-Omics: SCE and MAE to Python MuData
text: construct minimal CITE-seq dataset faithfully mimics real single-cell data: 300 cells, 1 000 genes (RNA), 40 surface proteins (ADT).
code:
set.seed(42)  n_cells    <- 300L n_genes    <- 1000L n_proteins <- 40L  cell_ids    <- paste0("cell_",    seq_len(n_cells)) gene_ids    <- paste0("gene_",    seq_len(n_genes)) protein_ids <- paste0("protein_", seq_len(n_proteins))  # ---- RNA counts (sparse, ~5% density) ----------------------------------- rna_mat <- Matrix::rsparsematrix(   nrow    = n_genes,   ncol    = n_cells,   density = 0.05,   repr    = "C" ) rna_mat <- round(abs(rna_mat) * 100)   # non-negative integer counts rownames(rna_mat) <- gene_ids colnames(rna_mat) <- cell_ids  # ---- ADT / protein counts (~50% density, lower diversity) --------------- adt_mat <- Matrix::rsparsematrix(   nrow    = n_proteins,   ncol    = n_cells,   density = 0.50,   repr    = "C" ) adt_mat <- round(abs(adt_mat) * 500) rownames(adt_mat) <- protein_ids colnames(adt_mat) <- cell_ids  # ---- Main SCE (RNA) ----------------------------------------------------- cell_types <- sample(c("CD4_T", "CD8_T", "B_cell", "Monocyte", "NK"),                      n_cells, replace = TRUE) batches    <- sample(c("batch1", "batch2"), n_cells, replace = TRUE)  sce <- SingleCellExperiment(   assays  = list(counts = rna_mat),   colData = DataFrame(     cell_type = cell_types,     batch     = batches,     n_counts  = Matrix::colSums(rna_mat)   ),   rowData = DataFrame(     gene_length = sample(500:5000, n_genes, replace = TRUE),     highly_variable = sample(c(TRUE, FALSE), n_genes,                              replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.1, 0.9))   ) )  # ---- ADT altExp -------------------------------------------------------- adt_sce <- SingleCellExperiment(   assays  = list(counts = adt_mat),   rowData = DataFrame(     isotype = sample(c("IgG", "IgM", "IgA"), n_proteins, replace = TRUE)   ) )  SingleCellExperiment::altExp(sce, "adt") <- adt_sce  sce #> class: SingleCellExperiment  #> dim: 1000 300  #> metadata(0): #> assays(1): counts #> rownames(1000): gene_1 gene_2 ... gene_999 gene_1000 #> rowData names(2): gene_length highly_variable #> colnames(300): cell_1 cell_2 ... cell_299 cell_300 #> colData names(3): cell_type batch n_counts #> reducedDimNames(0): #> mainExpName: NULL #> altExpNames(1): adt cat("Main SCE dimensions  : ", dim(sce)[1], "genes x", dim(sce)[2], "cells
") #> Main SCE dimensions  :  1000 genes x 300 cells cat("altExp names         : ", altExpNames(sce), "
") #> altExp names         :  adt cat("ADT dimensions       : ", dim(altExp(sce, "adt")), "
") #> ADT dimensions       :  40 300

title: Bridging Single-Cell Multi-Omics: SCE and MAE to Python MuData
text: sce_to_reticulate_mudata() maps main SCE one modality altExp modality. conversion zero-copy: sparse matrices remain original R memory locations. mdata thin R6 proxy around Python mudata.MuData object. data live Python; R just holds pointer.
code: mdata <- sce_to_reticulate_mudata(   x             = sce,   main_modality = "rna",   assay         = "counts",   altexp_assay  = "counts" ) mdata #> ReticulateMuData #>   n_obs x n_vars : 300 x 1040 #>   modalities (2) : rna, adt #>     rna:           300 obs x 1000 vars #>     adt:           300 obs x 40 vars #>   obs keys       : rna:cell_type, rna:batch, rna:n_counts #>   obsm keys      : rna, adt

code: library(MultiAssayExperiment)  # Reuse the same RNA and protein matrices from above sce_rna <- SingleCellExperiment(   assays  = list(counts = rna_mat),   colData = DataFrame(cell_type = cell_types, batch = batches) )  sce_adt <- SingleCellExperiment(   assays  = list(counts = adt_mat) )  # Sample map: each experiment has identical primary ↔ colname make_sm <- function(primary, colname, assay) {   DataFrame(assay = assay, primary = primary, colname = colname) }  smap <- rbind(   make_sm(cell_ids, cell_ids, "rna"),   make_sm(cell_ids, cell_ids, "adt") )  col_df <- DataFrame(   donor     = sample(paste0("donor_", 1:3), n_cells, replace = TRUE),   row.names = cell_ids )  mae <- MultiAssayExperiment(   experiments = ExperimentList(rna = sce_rna, adt = sce_adt),   colData     = col_df,   sampleMap   = smap )  mae #> A MultiAssayExperiment object of 2 listed #>  experiments with user-defined names and respective classes. #>  Containing an ExperimentList class object of length 2: #>  [1] rna: SingleCellExperiment with 1000 rows and 300 columns #>  [2] adt: SingleCellExperiment with 40 rows and 300 columns #> Functionality: #>  experiments() - obtain the ExperimentList instance #>  colData() - the primary/phenotype DataFrame #>  sampleMap() - the sample coordination DataFrame #>  `$`, `[`, `[[` - extract colData columns, subset, or experiment #>  *Format() - convert into a long or wide DataFrame #>  assays() - convert ExperimentList to a SimpleList of matrices #>  exportClass() - save data to flat files

title: Zero-copy sparse bridge: SingleCellExperiment → scipy.sparse → AnnData
text: vignette explains MofaflexR converts SingleCellExperiment (SCE) assay stored Matrix::dgCMatrix Python scipy.sparse.csc_matrix — without copying underlying data arrays — wraps anndata.AnnData object accessible R via anndataR::ReticulateAnnData.

title: Zero-copy sparse bridge: SingleCellExperiment → scipy.sparse → AnnData
text: Single-cell RNA-seq count matrices can contain tens millions non-zero entries. Copying matrices R ↔︎ Python boundary wastes memory time. MofaflexR avoids using reticulate::np_array(), exposes R’s contiguous memory NumPy via C buffer protocol without allocating new heap storage.

title: Zero-copy sparse bridge: SingleCellExperiment → scipy.sparse → AnnData
text: dgCMatrix m genes (rows) n cells (columns) stores: exactly CSC (Compressed Sparse Column) format used scipy.sparse.csc_matrix.

title: Zero-copy sparse bridge: SingleCellExperiment → scipy.sparse → AnnData
text: reticulate::np_array() uses C buffer protocol hand pointer existing R memory NumPy. resulting arrays flags.owndata == False, confirming views, copies.
code: # Pseudocode (simplified) sp         <- reticulate::import("scipy.sparse", convert = FALSE) py_data    <- reticulate::np_array(mat@x, dtype = "float64")  # no copy py_indices <- reticulate::np_array(mat@i, dtype = "int32")    # no copy py_indptr  <- reticulate::np_array(mat@p, dtype = "int32")    # no copy shape      <- reticulate::tuple(nr, nc) csc        <- sp$csc_matrix(reticulate::tuple(py_data, py_indices, py_indptr),                              shape = shape)

title: Zero-copy sparse bridge: SingleCellExperiment → scipy.sparse → AnnData
text: dgCMatrix genes × cells (R convention: rows features). AnnData uses obs × vars = cells × genes. call csc.T construction:
code: # csc.T is a csr_matrix of shape (cells, genes) # scipy computes this by swapping the CSC ↔ CSR interpretation; # no values are re-ordered — flags.owndata remains False.

title: Zero-copy sparse bridge: SingleCellExperiment → scipy.sparse → AnnData
text: Python csc_matrix holds borrowed pointer R memory. Ensure: source dgCMatrix remains alive (referenced R) Python uses . R code triggers copy--modify vector. R garbage collector freed object. typical single-session usage (SCE → AnnData → model fit → result back R) always satisfied.

title: Zero-copy sparse bridge: SingleCellExperiment → scipy.sparse → AnnData
code:
library(MofaflexR) library(SingleCellExperiment) library(Matrix)  set.seed(1) counts <- rsparsematrix(500, 100, density = 0.05, repr = "C") counts <- abs(round(counts)) sce    <- SingleCellExperiment(assays = list(counts = counts)) colnames(sce) <- paste0("cell", seq_len(ncol(sce))) rownames(sce) <- paste0("gene", seq_len(nrow(sce)))  # --- zero-copy CSC matrix (genes x cells) --- csc <- sce_assay_to_scipy_csc(sce) cat("owndata:", reticulate::py_to_r(csc$data$flags$owndata), "
") # owndata: FALSE  # --- full AnnData (cells x genes) --- rada     <- sce_to_reticulate_anndata(sce) py_adata <- rada$py_anndata() sp       <- reticulate::import("scipy.sparse", convert = FALSE) cat("Shape:   ", unlist(reticulate::py_to_r(py_adata$shape)), "
") cat("Sparse:  ", reticulate::py_to_r(sp$issparse(py_adata$X)), "
") cat("owndata: ", reticulate::py_to_r(py_adata$X$data$flags$owndata), "
") # Shape:    100 500 # Sparse:   TRUE # owndata:  FALSE

title: Fit MOFAFlex Models from R via basilisk and reticulate
text: fit_mofaflex() converts SCE (existing ReticulateAnnData / ReticulateMuData) required Python data structure, builds trains MOFA-FLEX model, optionally saves result MOFA2-compatible HDF5 format. Tip. group_by specified data single AnnData, fit_mofaflex() automatically wraps mudata.MuData promotes grouping column top-level obs. wrapped modality named rna automatically.
code: library(MofaflexR) library(muscData)   # BiocManager::install("muscData") library(scran);  library(scuttle)  # Build SCE and normalise (see vignette for full QC) sce <- Kang18_8vs8() sce <- sce[, sce$multiplets == "singlet" & !is.na(sce$cell)] sce <- scuttle::logNormCounts(sce) hvgs <- scran::getTopHVGs(scran::modelGeneVar(sce), n = 3000) sce_hvg <- sce[hvgs, ]  # Zero-copy bridge to Python AnnData adata <- sce_to_reticulate_anndata(sce_hvg, assay = "logcounts")  # Train MOFA-FLEX with two groups (ctrl / stim) # Passing a single AnnData with group_by automatically wraps it in MuData. model_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".hdf5") model <- fit_mofaflex(   data             = adata,   data_options     = list(group_by           = "stim",                           scale_per_group    = TRUE,                           subset_var         = NULL,                           plot_data_overview = FALSE),   model_options    = list(n_factors    = 10L,                           weight_prior = "Horseshoe"),   training_options = list(max_epochs             = 5000L,                           early_stopper_patience = 50L,                           seed                   = 42L,                           device                 = "cpu",                           save_path              = model_path),   mofa_compat      = "full"   # MOFA2-readable HDF5 )  # Load into MOFA2 for downstream analysis mofa <- load_mofaflex_model(model_path) MOFA2::plot_variance_explained(mofa)

title: Fit MOFAFlex Models from R via basilisk and reticulate

title: Fit MOFAFlex Models from R via basilisk and reticulate
code: library(MofaflexR) library(reticulate)  # Import Python modules (convert = FALSE keeps objects Python-native) mu  <- py_mudata()                             # mudata module ad  <- import("anndata",       convert = FALSE) sp  <- import("scipy.sparse",  convert = FALSE)  # Build two AnnData objects X1  <- sp$csr_matrix(r_to_py(matrix(1:12, 3L, 4L))) X2  <- sp$csr_matrix(r_to_py(matrix(1:6,  3L, 2L))) a1  <- ad$AnnData(X = X1) a2  <- ad$AnnData(X = X2) a1$obs_names <- r_to_py(c("obs1", "obs2", "obs3")) a2$obs_names <- r_to_py(c("obs1", "obs2", "obs3"))  # Combine into MuData py_md <- mu$MuData(py_dict(list("rna" = a1, "prot" = a2), convert = FALSE))  # Wrap in ReticulateMuData mdata <- ReticulateMuData$new(py_md) print(mdata) # ReticulateMuData #   n_obs     : 3 #   modalities: rna, prot #     rna:           3 obs x 4 vars #     prot:          3 obs x 2 vars  # Access a modality – returns ReticulateAnnData automatically rna <- mdata[["rna"]] inherits(rna, "ReticulateAnnData")   # TRUE rna$n_vars()                         # 4L  # Assign a new modality (no data copy) mdata[["rna"]] <- new_rna_anndata   # accepts ReticulateAnnData or Python AnnData  # Call a Python method with automatic return-type wrapping mdata_copy <- mdata$py_call("copy") inherits(mdata_copy, "ReticulateMuData")  # TRUE  # Access the raw Python object for muon/scanpy functions py_obj <- mdata$py_mudata() # muon.tl.mofa(py_obj, ...)

title: Fit MOFAFlex Models from R via basilisk and reticulate
text: dgCMatrix stores non-zero data three contiguous C arrays: reticulate::np_array(<R vector>, dtype = ...) exposes underlying R memory NumPy via C buffer protocol — data allocation occurs (numpy.ndarray.flags.owndata == False). three arrays passed directly scipy.sparse.csc_matrix((data, indices, indptr), shape=...). scipy copy buffers construction time; wraps reference. resulting csc_matrix (shape genes × cells) transposed via .T obtain csr_matrix (shape cells × genes) suitable AnnData’s obs × vars layout. scipy transpose also zero-copy: reuses C arrays, changing strides shape metadata.

title: Train a MOFAFLEX model — fit_mofaflex
text: data One : ReticulateMuData object (created sce_to_reticulate_mudata(), mae_to_reticulate_mudata(), reticulate_mudata()). ReticulateAnnData object anndataR package (created sce_to_reticulate_anndata()). Python mudata.MuData object (convert = FALSE). Python anndata.AnnData object (convert = FALSE). named R list named lists, outer names group names, inner names view names, leaf Python AnnData object. Example: list(ctrl = list(rna = adata_ctrl), stim = list(rna = adata_stim)). model_options Named R list model options passed mofaflex.ModelOptions(...). See Model options section available fields defaults. data_options Named R list data options passed mofaflex.DataOptions(...). See Data options section. training_options Named R list training options passed mofaflex.TrainingOptions(...). mofa_compat field always overridden top-level mofa_compat argument. See Training options section. mofa_compat Controls MOFA2 compatibility saved model file. One : "modelonly" (default) — save model weights, readable MOFA2. "full" — save full model state. FALSE — disable MOFA2-compatible saving entirely. value injected training_options$mofa_compat Python options object constructed, overriding user-supplied value. smooth_options Optional named R list smooth options passed mofaflex.SmoothOptions(...), required MEFISTO / GP-factor model. NULL (default) omits smooth term entirely. See Smooth options section.

title: Convert a MultiAssayExperiment to a ReticulateMuData — mae_to_reticulate_mudata
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # ---- Tiny MAE with two experiments ---- library(MultiAssayExperiment) library(SingleCellExperiment) library(Matrix) library(S4Vectors)  set.seed(1) n_samples <- 50 sample_ids <- paste0("sample_", seq_len(n_samples))  rna_mat  <- Matrix::rsparsematrix(200, n_samples, 0.1, repr = "C") rownames(rna_mat) <- paste0("gene_", seq_len(200)) colnames(rna_mat) <- sample_ids  adt_mat  <- Matrix::rsparsematrix(30, n_samples, 0.4, repr = "C") rownames(adt_mat) <- paste0("protein_", seq_len(30)) colnames(adt_mat) <- sample_ids  sce_rna  <- SingleCellExperiment(assays = list(counts = rna_mat)) sce_adt  <- SingleCellExperiment(assays = list(counts = adt_mat))  sample_df <- S4Vectors::DataFrame(   condition = sample(c("ctrl", "treat"), n_samples, TRUE),   row.names = sample_ids )  el <- MultiAssayExperiment::ExperimentList(rna = sce_rna, adt = sce_adt) cl <- MultiAssayExperiment::listToMap(         setNames(lapply(c("rna","adt"), function(nm) {           data.frame(primary = sample_ids, colname = sample_ids)         }), c("rna","adt"))       ) mae <- MultiAssayExperiment::MultiAssayExperiment(   experiments = el,   colData     = sample_df,   sampleMap   = cl )  mdata <- mae_to_reticulate_mudata(mae) print(mdata) mdata$n_obs() mdata[["rna"]] mdata[["adt"]] } # }

title: Convert a SingleCellExperiment assay to a scipy CSC sparse matrix — sce_assay_to_scipy_csc
text: Extracts assay SingleCellExperiment::SingleCellExperiment object, coerces Matrix::dgCMatrix, constructs Python scipy.sparse.csc_matrix passing reticulate::np_array() views underlying R memory (true zero-copy: flags.owndata = FALSE).

title: Convert a SingleCellExperiment assay to a scipy CSC sparse matrix — sce_assay_to_scipy_csc
text: reticulate::np_array() uses C buffer protocol expose R's contiguous memory NumPy without copying (flags.owndata = FALSE). works plain R vectors already C-contiguous: R numeric (double) maps directly NumPy float64. R integer (int32_t) maps directly NumPy int32. Note np.array(..., copy=False): NumPy >= 2.0 changed flag raise ValueError copy needed.  Using reticulate::np_array() avoids entirely canonical reticulate API zero-copy array creation R. Lifetime caveat: returned Python object holds borrowed reference underlying R vectors.  modify, reallocate, allow mat@x, mat@, mat@p vectors garbage-collected Python object alive.

text: Converts SingleCellExperiment::SingleCellExperiment (possibly alternative experiments attached via SingleCellExperiment::altExps()) ReticulateMuData. main SCE mapped modality named main_modality.  alternative experiment (accessed SingleCellExperiment::altExpNames()) mapped modality using altExp name key. matrix transfers zero-copy (via sce_assay_to_scipy_csc()).

title: Convert a SingleCellExperiment to a ReticulateMuData — sce_to_reticulate_mudata
text: x SingleCellExperiment::SingleCellExperiment. main_modality Character string; name main SCE modality. Default: "rna". assay Character string; assay name use X main modality (passed sce_to_anndata()).  Default: "counts". altexp_assay Character string NULL.  Assay use X alternative experiment modality.  NULL (default) first available assay used altExp. obs Named list additional columns add modalities' obs data frames.  element must vector/factor length ncol(x). Forwarded sce_to_anndata() modality. obsm Named list embedding matrices (cells × dims) attach modality's obsm. Forwarded sce_to_anndata() main SCE. ... Additional arguments forwarded sce_to_anndata() every modality (including altExps).  Arguments differ modalities can set via obs, obsm, etc., apply main modality.

title: Convert a SingleCellExperiment to a ReticulateMuData — sce_to_reticulate_mudata
text: ReticulateMuData wrapping Python mudata.MuData.

title: Convert a SingleCellExperiment to a ReticulateMuData — sce_to_reticulate_mudata
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # ---- Minimal synthetic CITE-seq example ---- library(SingleCellExperiment) library(Matrix)  set.seed(42) n_cells <- 100; n_genes <- 500; n_proteins <- 30  rna_counts <- Matrix::rsparsematrix(n_genes, n_cells, density = 0.1,                                     repr = "C") adt_counts <- Matrix::rsparsematrix(n_proteins, n_cells, density = 0.5,                                     repr = "C")  sce <- SingleCellExperiment(   assays  = list(counts = rna_counts),   colData = S4Vectors::DataFrame(cell_type = sample(c("T","B"), n_cells, TRUE)) ) rownames(sce) <- paste0("gene_", seq_len(n_genes)) colnames(sce) <- paste0("cell_", seq_len(n_cells))  adt_sce <- SingleCellExperiment(assays = list(counts = adt_counts)) rownames(adt_sce) <- paste0("protein_", seq_len(n_proteins)) colnames(adt_sce) <- colnames(sce)  SingleCellExperiment::altExp(sce, "adt") <- adt_sce  mdata <- sce_to_reticulate_mudata(sce, main_modality = "rna", assay = "counts") print(mdata) mdata$obs_names   # shared cell barcodes mdata[["rna"]]    # ReticulateAnnData for RNA mdata[["adt"]]    # ReticulateAnnData for ADT } # }
